parameters.subject: Your AI Readiness Checklist — Emvy
parameters.content:
Hi {{ $json.first_name }},

Here's your AI Readiness Checklist. Answer the 10 questions honestly — and you'll know exactly where to start.

The 10 Questions:

  1. Do you spend more than 3 hours/week on repetitive admin tasks?
  2. Is your team manually entering data into multiple systems?
  3. Do you follow up with customers using notes, texts, or memory?
  4. Is your marketing the same for every customer?
  5. Do you have clear processes for your most time-consuming tasks?
  6. Would you know if an employee made a mistake in a key process?
  7. Do you have 30+ minutes per week to learn and implement new tools?
  8. Is your team comfortable using new software (not technical)?
  9. Do you have organized, accessible data about your customers?
  10. Do you have budget set aside for AI tools this quarter?

Scoring:
7-10 yes = Ready for AI
4-6 yes = Almost ready — fix 2-3 things first
0-3 yes = Start here before investing in AI

Reply to this email with your answers and I'll send you a personalized breakdown.

— Connor, Emvy

parameters.subject: The mistake most SMBs make with AI
parameters.content:
Hi {{ $json.first_name }},

Quick one today.

Most small businesses we talk to have the same problem: they're trying to use AI before they've figured out where their team is actually wasting time.

So they buy a tool, try it for a week, get half results, and quit.

Sound familiar?

The fix isn't the AI. It's knowing which process to automate first.

Take 2 minutes. Look at your last week. What's the one task you did that felt like Groundhog Day — the same thing over and over?

That's your starting point.

More on this in the next email.

— Connor

parameters.subject: What AI actually looks like in a real SMB
parameters.content:
Hi {{ $json.first_name }},

I ran an audit for a 12-person logistics company last month.

They were manually entering shipment data into three different systems. Same info, typed three times, every single day. One guy was spending 4 hours a day on it.

We found them a simple automation. Cost $0 to test. Took 2 hours to set up.

He gets that time back now. No layoffs. No new software licenses. Just wiring up the systems they already had.

That's usually where the highest ROI lives — not in the shiny new AI tools. In the stuff you're already doing manually that should be automatic.

What's your version of that story?

— Connor

parameters.subject: Want a deeper look at your business?
parameters.content:
Hi {{ $json.first_name }},

If you've been answering the checklist questions — or just thinking about them — here's an offer that might be useful.

We do a free 15-minute audit call. Not a pitch. Not a sales call.

You answer 10 quick questions about your business before we talk. I review them. Then we spend 15 minutes on a real call where I tell you specifically where AI could save you time — or where you'd be wasting your money trying.

Most people find it useful even if they don't work with us.

No obligation. No follow-up sequences if you don't want them.

parameters.subject: Last one from me — and what to do next
parameters.content:
Hi {{ $json.first_name }},

This is my last email in the sequence. I don't want to clutter your inbox.

If you're still on the fence — here's what I'd say:

The businesses that get the most out of AI are the ones that started before they felt ready. They picked one small thing, tested it, learned, adjusted.

They didn't wait for certainty.

If you want a no-pressure conversation about where AI might fit in your business, I'm here. If not — good luck with everything.

Either way — the checklist questions are worth sitting with. Even answering them for yourself is useful.

Best,
Connor

P.S. If you ever want to book that audit call: https://calendly.com/emvyai
